zh.lifeSummary: 當Susanna Payne於1584年出生在英國素福爾可霍斯亞德時，她的父親William Payne III27歲，母親Agnes Neves22歲。她於大約1612年與Thomas Page在英國素福爾可霍斯亞德結婚。他們至少是14個兒子和10個女兒的父母。她去世於1631年8月1日在她的家鄉享年47歲，埋葬在英國素可渥頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Susanna Payne于1584年出生在英国素福爾可霍斯亞德时，她的父亲William Payne III27岁，母亲Agnes Neves22岁。她于大约1612年与Thomas Page在英国素福爾可霍斯亞德结婚。他们至少是14个儿子和10个女儿的父母。她去世于1631年8月1日在她的家乡享年47岁，埋葬在英国素可渥頓。

nameMeanings.name: Payne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen (from Latin Paganus), a fairly common personal name among Normans. It derived from a word that originally meant ‘villager, rustic’, later ‘heathen’, but it had doubtless lost these connotations in its use as a late medieval personal name. This name has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

History: Thomas Payne, who was a freeman of Plymouth Colony in 1639, was the founder of a large American family, which included Robert Treat Paine (1731–1814), one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. See also   Paine  .

variants.name: Payn
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Payen
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Pagen, Paien, from Latin Paganus, from paganus ‘country-dweller’, a derivative of pagus ‘of or relating to the countryside’, possibly given to a child not yet baptized (because of its later sense of ‘pagan, heathen’). Alternatively, a nickname for a country-born person. This surname is also found in the West Indies (mainly Haiti) and Mauritius.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Pagan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Angel, Carlos, Rafael, Ramon, Pedro, Francisco, Jesus, Miguel, Julio.

Spanish and Catalan: Castilianized form (Pagán) of Catalan Pagà, from the Late Latin personal name Paganus, which as a derivative of Latin pagus ‘outlying village’ originally meant ‘villager’ (see Payne ). Alternatively, in some cases, a topographic name from Catalan pagà ‘heath’ (from the same Latin word pagus).
English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen, from Late Latin Paganus (see Payne , compare 1 above).

variants.name: Paynes
meanings: English: variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Paine
meanings: English: variant of   Payne  .

History: The author of the republican treatise The Rights of Man, Thomas Paine (1737–1809), left England for North America in the mid 1770s, where he became involved in the movement that led to independence. His pamphlet of 1776, Common Sense, influenced the Declaration of Independence and furnished some of the arguments justifying it.

variants.name: Pank
meanings: English (Norfolk): perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Pank(e). It could be a shortened form of Pancok, which is probably a pet form of Pane (see Payne ).
Sorbian: nickname from a diminutive of pan ‘mister, lord, landlord’ (see Pan ). Compare Panke .

variants.name: Pane
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Aldo, Carmela, Carmine, Ciro, Concetta, Enzo, Giuseppe, Mario, Armando, Claudio, Macario, Remigio.

Italian: from pane ‘bread’, used as a metonymic occupational name for a baker or as a nickname meaning ‘good thing’, and also as a personal name. This surname is also found in southern France and Corsica.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Panes
meanings: English (Somerset): variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Pannell
meanings: English (southeastern):
possibly from Middle English panel, panel(l)e ‘cloth, saddle-cloth, saddle’ or ‘pane (of glass)’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such items.
from the Middle English personal name Paynel (Old French Payenel, Latin Paganellus), a pet form of Payne (from Latin Paganus).

variants.name: Penson
meanings: English:
patronymic from the Middle English female personal name Penn, a pet form of Pernel, or perhaps from the Middle English personal name Payne + -son. See Penn , Parnell .

habitational name from Penson in North Huish (Devon), which derives from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Payn(e) (see Payne ) + Middle English toun ‘village, town, settlement’ (Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’).

nameMeanings.name: Susanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament form (Luke 8:3) of the Hebrew name Shoshana (from shoshan ‘lily’, which in modern Hebrew also means ‘rose’).

variants.name: Suzannah
meanings: New Testament form (Luke 8:3) of the Hebrew name Shoshana (from shoshan ‘lily’, which in modern Hebrew also means ‘rose’).

variants.name: Suzanna
meanings: New Testament form (Luke 8:3) of the Hebrew name Shoshana (from shoshan ‘lily’, which in modern Hebrew also means ‘rose’).

variants.name: Susana
meanings: From Hebrew. See Susanna .

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Susanna Payne was born in 1584, in Hawstead, Suffolk, England, her father, William Payne III, was 27 and her mother, Agnes Neves, was 22. She married Thomas Page about 1612, in Hawstead, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 14 sons and 10 daughters. She died on 1 August 1631, in her hometown, at the age of 47, and was buried in Waldron, Sussex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Susanna Payne est née en 1584, à Hawstead, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, William Payne III, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Agnes Neves, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Page environ 1612, à Hawstead, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 14 fils et 10 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 août 1631, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterrée à Waldron, Sussex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Susanna Payne 1584 in Hawstead, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Payne III 27 und ihre Mutter Agnes Neves 22. Sie hat um 1612 in Hawstead, Suffolk, England Thomas Page geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 14 Söhne und 10 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. August 1631 im Alter von 47 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Waldron, Sussex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Susanna Payne è nata il nel 1584 nel Hawstead, Suffolk, England, sua padre, William Payne III, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Agnes Neves, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Page circa 1612, nel Hawstead, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 14 figli e 10 figlie. Morì il 1 agosto 1631, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 47 anni, e fu sepolta a Waldron, Sussex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Susanna Payneが1584年にイギリススフォルクハウステアドで生まれた時，父William Payne IIIは27歳，母Agnes Nevesは22歳であった。約1612年にイギリススフォルクハウステアドでThomas Pageと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも14人の息子と10人の娘の親であった。彼女は1631年8月1日に故郷で47歳で死去し，イギリススセックスワルドロンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Susanna Payne(이)가 1584년 영국 수폴크 호스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Payne III(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Neves(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1612년 영국 수폴크 호스테아드에서 Thomas Page(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 열 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1631년 8월 1일 그녀의 고향에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수세스 월드런에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Susanna Payne nasceu em 1584, em Hawstead, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Payne III, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Neves, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Page aproximadamente 1612, em Hawstead, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 14 filhos e 10 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de agosto de 1631, em sua cidade natal, com 47 anos, e foi sepultada em Waldron, Sussex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Susanna Payne родилась в 1584 г. в местности хавстид, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени William Payne III было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Neves, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Page около 1612 г. в местности хавстид, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 14 сыновей и 10 дочерей. Она умерла 1 августа 1631 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 47 лет и была похоронена в местности валдрон, суссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Susanna Payne nació en 1584, en Hawstead, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, William Payne III, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Agnes Neves, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Thomas Page aproximadamente 1612, en Hawstead, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 14 hijos y 10 hijas. Murió el 1 de agosto de 1631, en su pueblo, a la edad de 47 años, y fue sepultada en Waldron, Sussex, Inglaterra.
